items.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
items.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

items.title: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots snapping pictures, report finds
items.summary: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

items.title: Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits

items.title: Virginia approves redistricting measure, boosting Democrats' hopes for midterms
items.summary: The new maps approved by the state's voters could allow Democrats to flip as many as four House seats.

items.title: From Epstein to sock puppets: Key takeaways from Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing
items.summary: He denied making a deal with Donald Trump on interest rates and backed "policy regime change" at the central bank.

items.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel

items.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon

items.title: Domestic workers legally recognised in Indonesia after '22-year struggle'

items.title: Zelensky says failure of US envoys to visit Kyiv is 'disrespectful'
items.summary: US envoy Steve Witkoff and Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner have travelled to Moscow several times, but never to Kyiv.

items.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
items.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

items.title: Mass trial for 486 alleged gang members begins in El Salvador

items.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
items.summary: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

items.title: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume

items.title: Role of US officials killed in crash in Mexico under scrutiny
items.summary: Two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

items.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl

items.title: Arrests in Nigeria after abduction of local monarch 'held for ransom'
items.summary: The kidnappers raided his palace on Saturday and are believed to be holding him in a forest.

items.summary: The new proposal would involve the publication licensing Alex Jones's company, which faces liquidation.

items.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

items.title: Indian politicians are campaigning while holding fish. What is going on?
items.summary: In West Bengal polls, fish moves from plate to politics, symbolising identity and cultural pride.

items.title: Victory slips away as marathon runner celebrates too soon
